p1|JUS.summary: Perception of change in national economic situation compared to one year ago (better or worse).

p2|JUS.summary: Word choice to describe the current political situation of the country.

p18|JUS.summary: Agreement with security forces killing organized crime members instead of detaining them for justice.

p5_1|JUS.summary: Willingness to disagree with parents when respondent believes they are right.

p6_1|JUS.summary: Opinion on who should set limits on people's behavior (first mention).

p7|JUS.summary: Reasons why respondent obeys the laws.

p8|JUS.summary: Perceived respect for laws by government officials in Mexico (0-10 scale).

p9|JUS.summary: Perceived respect for laws by citizens in Mexico (0-10 scale).

p10|JUS.summary: Respondent's self-reported respect for the law (0-10 scale).

p11_1|JUS.summary: Opinion on who violates laws most frequently (first mention).

p12|JUS.summary: Self-assessed knowledge of personal rights and obligations.

p14|JUS.summary: Most effective method to change an unjust or harmful government decision.

p16|JUS.summary: Least effective method to change an unjust or harmful government decision.

p17|JUS.summary: Agreement that community members have the right to punish a killer if authorities do nothing.

p19|JUS.summary: Opinion on what is worse for society: justice system errors or other specified outcomes.

p20_1|JUS.summary: Most important priority regarding justice (first choice).

p20_2|JUS.summary: Second most important priority regarding justice.

p21|JUS.summary: Phrase that best describes justice according to respondent.

p22|JUS.summary: Opinion on whether justice in Mexico functions well or poorly.

p25a_1|JUS.summary: Opinion on whether police resolve problems fairly.

p27|JUS.summary: Preference between two approaches when two people have a dispute.

p28|JUS.summary: Action respondent would take if needing legal defense without money for a lawyer.

p29|JUS.summary: Knowledge of constitutional provisions regarding justice administered by judges and magistrates.

p30|JUS.summary: Action respondent would take if a judge's sentence negatively affects their interests.

p32|JUS.summary: Opinion on how much oral trials will improve justice administration in Mexico.

p33|JUS.summary: Opinion on whether all people receive equal treatment in courts or if money and connections determine outcomes.

p34|JUS.summary: Knowledge of what amparo (constitutional remedy) is.

p34_1|JUS.summary: Knowledge of the purpose and function of amparo.

p35|JUS.summary: Opinion on how much amparo serves to defend people's rights.

p36|JUS.summary: Belief in whether it is possible to sue the government for damages in Mexico.

p37|JUS.summary: Perceived likelihood of winning a lawsuit against the government.

p39|JUS.summary: Opinion on whether it is worthwhile to go to court to file a lawsuit.

p40_1|JUS.summary: Expectation that courts and judges would be respectful in treatment.

p40_1_1|JUS.summary: Expectation that the Public Ministry would be respectful in treatment.

p40_2_1|JUS.summary: Expectation that police would be respectful in treatment.

p41|JUS.summary: Opinion on how independent judges and magistrates are in making their own decisions.

p42_1|JUS.summary: Agreement that judges and magistrates generally guarantee everyone a fair trial.

p43|JUS.summary: Preference for whether judge sentences should be just or strictly follow the law.

p44|JUS.summary: Opinion on whether President should follow a judge's decision blocking a desired airport project.

p45|JUS.summary: Respondent's reaction to a judge's guilty verdict in a criminal case.

p46|JUS.summary: Opinion on whether sentences from the Supreme Court of Justice are fair or unfair.

p47|JUS.summary: Opinion on who should appoint judges and magistrates.

p48_1|JUS.summary: Credibility of guilt claims heard on radio.

p48_2|JUS.summary: Credibility of guilt claims read in newspapers.

p48_3|JUS.summary: Credibility of guilt claims seen on television.

p48_4|JUS.summary: Credibility of guilt claims read on social media.

p48_5|JUS.summary: Credibility of guilt claims made by social organizations.

p48_6|JUS.summary: Credibility of guilt when a judge issues a guilty verdict.

p49|JUS.summary: Opinion on who should sanction a judge or magistrate for misconduct during a trial.

p50|JUS.summary: Opinion on whether judges and magistrates who act improperly receive punishment or go unpunished.

p51|JUS.summary: Opinion on whether judges in Mexico are well or poorly paid for their work.

p53|JUS.summary: Experience hiring or requesting legal services from a lawyer.

p54|JUS.summary: Preference between paying a lawyer or resolving a legal problem independently.

p55|JUS.summary: Opinion on whether a lawyer must know the judge to win a legal case.

p56|JUS.summary: Opinion on how well public defenders defend accused persons who cannot afford a lawyer.

p57|JUS.summary: Agreement that lawyers generally practice their profession honestly.

p58|JUS.summary: Opinion on appropriate percentage fee lawyers should charge from recovered client funds.

p59|JUS.summary: Opinion on how to prevent police abuse and improve their work during investigations.

p60_1|JUS.summary: Experience of theft in the last five years and whether the problem was resolved.

p61_3|JUS.summary: Resolution of vehicle damage payment dispute.

p61_5|JUS.summary: Resolution of unjustified job termination problem.

p61_6|JUS.summary: Resolution of neighbor property damage problem.

p61_9|JUS.summary: Resolution of school authority or teacher problems.

p61_12|JUS.summary: Resolution of unpaid child support problem.

p61_13|JUS.summary: Resolution of child-related problems after separation from partner.

p61_14|JUS.summary: Resolution of inheritance dispute with family members.

p61_16|JUS.summary: Resolution of tenant non-payment of rent problem.

p61_17|JUS.summary: Resolution of unresolved government petition or administrative procedure.
